{| class="wikitable collapsible collapsed" style="text-align: center;font-size:90%;"
|-
!colspan=6 style="background: #FF0000; color: #FFFFFF;|SEA Games history
|-
!style="text-align: center;"|First match
|style="text-align:center;"|{{fb|MAS}} 1–2 {{fb-rt|IDN}}<br>(19 November 1977; [[Kuala Lumpur]], [[Malaysia]])
|-
!style="text-align: center;"|Last match
|style="text-align:center;"|{{fb|IDN}} 0–0 (4–3 pen.) {{fb-rt|SGP}}<br>(14 August 1999; [[Bandar Seri Begawan]], [[Brunei]])
|-
!style="text-align: center;"|Biggest win
|style="text-align:center;"|{{fb|IDN}} 10–0 {{fb-rt|CAM}}<br>(6 December 1995; [[Thailand]])
|-
!style="text-align: center;"|Biggest defeat
|style="text-align:center;"|{{fb|THA}} 7–0 {{fb-rt|IDN}}<br>(15 December 1985; [[Bangkok]], [[Thailand]])
|-
!style="text-align: center;"|Best result
|style="text-align:center;"|'''Gold medalist''' ([[Football at the 1987 Southeast Asian Games|1987]], [[Football at the 1991 Southeast Asian Games|1991]])
|-
!style="text-align: center;"|Worst result
|style="text-align:center;"|'''Group stage''' ([[Football at the 1983 Southeast Asian Games|1983]], [[Football at the 1995 Southeast Asian Games|1995]])
|}

==Head-to-head record==
:''As of 9 June 2026 after match against [[Mozambique national football team|Mozambique]].''<ref name="RSSSF" /><ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.worldfootball.net/teams/te1050/indonesia/|title=Indonesia - Historical results|website=World Football|date=27 December 2019 |access-date=28 June 2021|archive-date=26 November 2021|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20211126213408/https://www.worldfootball.net/teams/indonesien-team/21/|url-status=live}}</ref><ref name="11v11">{{cite web|url=https://www.11v11.com/teams/indonesia/tab/stats/|title=Indonesia national football team statistics and records: all-time record|publisher=AFS Enterprises |access-date=18 October 2023}}</ref>{{efn|group=infobox|Does not include B team, selection, junior, club, etc.}}

==Honours==
===Continental===
* '''[[Football at the Asian Games|Asian Games]]'''
** {{bronze3}} Bronze medal (1): [[Football at the 1958 Asian Games|1958]]

===Regional===
* '''[[ASEAN Championship]]'''
** {{silver2}} Runners-up (6): [[2000 AFF Championship|2000]], [[2002 AFF Championship|2002]], [[2004 AFF Championship|2004]], [[2010 AFF Championship|2010]], [[2016 AFF Championship|2016]], [[2020 AFF Championship|2020]]
** {{bronze3}} Third place (1): [[1998 AFF Championship|1998]]

* '''[[Football at the SEA Games|SEA Games]]'''
** {{gold1}} '''Gold medal (2):''' [[Football at the 1987 SEA Games|1987]], [[Football at the 1991 SEA Games|1991]]
** {{silver2}} Silver medal (2):  [[Football at the 1979 SEA Games|1979]], [[Football at the 1997 SEA Games|1997]]
** {{bronze3}} Bronze medal (3): [[Football at the 1981 SEA Games|1981]], [[Football at the 1999 SEA Games|1989]], [[Football at the 1999 SEA Games|1999]]

* '''[[Far Eastern Championship Games]]'''
** {{silver2}} Silver medal (1): [[Football at the 1934 Far Eastern Championship Games|1934]] as ''[[Dutch East Indies national football team results|Dutch East Indies]]''

===Friendly===
* '''Garuda Championship Series'''
**{{gold1}} '''Champions (1):''' 2026
* '''[[FIFA Series]]'''
**{{silver2}} '''Runners-up (1):''' [[2026 FIFA Series (men's matches)#Indonesia|2026]]

* '''[[Merdeka Tournament]]'''
** '''{{gold1}} Champions (3):''' 1961, 1962, 1969
**'''{{silver2}} Runners-up (2):''' 1957, 2006
**'''{{bronze3}} Third place (1): '''1958

* '''[[South Vietnam Independence Cup]]'''
**'''{{silver2}} Runners-up (1): '''1962

* '''[[Aga Khan Gold Cup]]'''
** '''{{gold1}} Champions (1):''' 1961

* '''[[King's Cup (Thailand)|King's Cup]]'''
** '''{{gold1}} Champions (1):''' 1968
**'''{{silver2}} Runners-up (2): '''1969, 1984

* '''[[Jakarta Anniversary Tournament]]'''
** '''{{gold1}} Champions (1):''' 1972
**'''{{silver2}} Runners-up (3): '''1973, 1974, 1978

* '''Pesta Sukan'''
** '''{{gold1}} Champions (1):''' 1972

* '''Korean Cup'''
**'''{{silver2}} Runners-up (2): '''1972, 1980

*'''Merlion Cup'''
**'''{{bronze3}} Third place (1): '''1982

* '''[[Indonesian Independence Cup]]'''
** '''{{gold1}} Champions (3):''' 1987, 2000, 2008
**'''{{silver2}} Runners-up (2): '''1986, 1994

* '''[[ASEAN All-Stars|ASEAN All-Stars]]'''
** '''{{gold1}} Champions (1):''' 2014

*'''Myanmar Grand Royal Challenge Cup'''
** '''{{silver2}} Runner-up (1):''' 2008

*'''SCTV Cup'''
** '''{{silver2}} Runner-up (1):''' 2012

*'''Al Nakba Cup'''
** '''{{bronze3}} Third Place (1):''' 2012
